title: psorospermum stenophyllum
intro: Psorospermum stenophyllum, also known as the poison tree, is a plant species belonging to the family Guttiferae. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of a toxic compound called plumbagin. The health benefits associated with Psorospermum stenophyllum include its potential to treat skin conditions and wounds, as well as its antimicrobial properties that can help combat infections. Herbal preparations of the plant, often in the form of extracts or tinctures, are used in traditional medicine to leverage its medicinal properties, although caution is advised due to its toxic nature.
